figures.caption: Figure 9: ART-E (Email Search) training curves for Qwen-3-14B (moving average; window size 10).

Figure 1: Example of recursive agent inference on a deep research or travel-planning task where agents can decide to spawn and delegate sub-problems to sub-agents. This produces a dynamically structured and recursive execution tree where each node corresponds to one agent instance attempting to solve an assigned task. RAO optimizes a single policy to act across all levels tree, teaching it to best take advantage of recursive inference.

Table 1: TEXTCRAFT-SYNTH results across evaluation difficulties. Success rate (SR) is computed over the evaluation set. Steps and wall-clock time are computed over the intersection of tasks successfully solved by both methods at each difficulty.
Figure 4: TEXTCRAFT-SYNTH training curvess (moving average; window size 10).
Figure 2: RAO Reward Design. Each node receives a local reward from its own success and a delegation bonus from the success rate of its children. The example uses λ = 0.4.
Table 9: TEXTCRAFT-SYNTH token usage on the intersection of tasks solved by both single and recursive agents in the 40K/256K setting.
Table 10: OOLONG-REAL token usage on the intersection of tasks with non-zero score for both single and recursive agents.
Figure 5: OOLONG-REAL training curves (moving average; window size 10).
